channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish all over the street and overflowing bins, 28th June
item.description:
Please find photos of overflowing bins. Whole street is one bit rubbish bin which is extremely annoying and tiring especially in this heath. Can some serious steps be taken to finally resolve this issue? We have been raising the problem almost fortnightly now.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Approach, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Wooden furniture shelves

item.title: It’s parked on pavement outside my home, 28th June
item.description:
It’s parked outside my home on pavement Move it please

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Offord Road, Barnsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: SORN vehicle driven and abandoned in middle of night, front tyre completely gone. Driver was seen to be intoxicated getting in and out of vehicle. Driver of vehicle making a nuisance at 1am playing loud music and slamming doors. Was reported to Bromley out of hours team, but wasn't anyone available to attend at the time., 28th June
item.description:
I called the Bromley out of hours team at 1:30am to report noise nuisance. Man was getting in and out of the vehicle for at least 2 hours, playing music at full blast. He was getting in and out of the vehicle slamming his door and slamming the boot lid which kept opening. In daylight you can see the driver side tyre completely missing. No latch in boot lid. And car is marked as SORN on DVLA website. Car is not parked in bay, but off the kerb and outside the white markings. Man was clearly not with senses, and I presume by his behaviour and movement he was under influence of drink/drugs.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walpole Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A high traffic crossing that is dangerous to pedestrians, 28th June
item.description:
This junction should have a traffic light or zebra crossing as it is a very high traffic area and many pedestrians cross here. It’s currently very dangerous as there is no infrastructure in place for pedestrians to cross
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25-27, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Flytipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Romola Road, Tulse Hill, Greater London

item.title: Spillage down the road, 28th June
item.description:
Some sort of spillage going the whole way down the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cattistock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter & rats, 28th June
item.description:
Church walk is always dirty with litters , the tray for example on the photo has been there more than a week. The footpath is always dirty and due to this litter problem there are rats in the area as well, could you support to get our streets cleaned regularly please

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Walk, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement and kerbside rarely swept., 28th June
item.description:
Photo shows weeds removed from outside my house.. Build up of leaves along pavement and in the kerb.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Underhill Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hedge making it difficult to walk on pavement, 28th June
item.description:
The footway under the railway bridge is already dangerously narrow and with this prickly bramble hedge growing across it is very difficult to walk safely. Please could this receive some attention?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lincoln Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Overhanging the highway from private property

item.title: Low Hanging Branches, 28th June
item.description:
Please arrange to cut low hanging branches. This has been reported a few times several weeks ago and no action taken. Thanks Please contact me to discuss why action has not been taken as of yet.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): James Watt Way, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
